metadata.title: Why AI Help Made Students Worse
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Reallocation Engine candidate 09. Source chapter: books/the-reallocation-engine/chapters/97-fundamental-themes.md (the Frictional section only — struggle is the mechanism of learning).
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock

style_bible.visual_style: editorial paper collage on real newsprint scan; desaturated plates; flat annotation plane; serif labels with hairline underlines; hand-drawn annotation ring used once per graphic at most; two side-by-side cartoon brains as the hero object
style_bible.color_palette: cream ground, charcoal serif type, dusty navy = struggle / learning / the brain that fires and grows, crimson = the AI-assisted path / the dark unchanged brain / the exam drop, gold highlighter as the single editor's-pen voice

metadata.color_semantics: dusty navy #3D5A80 = struggle / learning / the brain that grows; crimson #BF3339 = the AI-assisted path / the dark brain / the exam drop; never swap mid-film
metadata.note: Card exclusions honored: NO EEG / Kosmyna methodology (the 55% connectivity stat and the EEG study are NOT used), NO BDNF biochemistry beyond naming a spark (never name dopamine/BDNF/dendritic spines — just 'a spark fires, connections grow'), NO phase-gate framework (no AI-handles-X/human-handles-Y gate), NO tier taxonomy (no Tiers 1-7), NO series/product plugs (no AI+1, Frictional, Boondoggling, Brutalist, no URLs). Two cartoon brains, one problem, compare move. Verified from ch.97 Frictional section: the Bastani finding — AI-during-practice group scored 48% HIGHER during practice and 17 percentage points LOWER on the unassisted exam; learning is a physical event triggered by a prediction error (cognitive struggle); remove the friction and you remove the trigger; 'the artifact was fine, the brain was unchanged'; struggle is not the price of learning, it is the mechanism; good AI makes the struggle more productive rather than removing it.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B11: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. One group of students scored forty-eight percent higher during practice, and seventeen points lower on the real exam. They had AI. Here's why it backfired.

card.copy: Why AI help made students worse

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [TITLE] AI help made students worse

beats.narration_text: Two groups, same problems, same hour. One worked through it by hand. The other let AI help. Guess which one felt like it learned more.
beats.scene_description: two students at desks side by side pinned to the newsprint ground — left works a problem by hand with scratch paper, right watches an AI auto-fill a worksheet on a laptop; slow push-in between them
beats.image_prompt: printed photo of two students at desks side by side, the left one solving a math problem by hand on scratch paper looking focused, the right one relaxed watching a laptop auto-complete a worksheet, editorial collage, pinned like a clipping to aged newsprint, desaturated flat print reproduction
beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] two students, by-hand vs AI-assisted — push-in

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [PHOTO] two students, by-hand vs AI-assisted — push-in

beats.narration_text: During practice, the AI group raced ahead. Forty-eight percent higher. Cleaner work, faster answers. It looked exactly like mastery.

production_viz.label: practice: AI group +48%
production_viz.mechanic: two bars under a 'practice' label; the crimson AI-group bar grows well above the navy struggle-group bar; a '+48%' tag on the crimson bar
production_viz.note: 48% higher in practice — verbatim from ch.97. The seductive result.

beats.narration_text: But learning isn't a feeling. It's a physical event in the brain, and feeling fluent is not the same as the event happening.
beats.scene_description: the core distinction as a large serif quote card; gold highlighter sweeps 'learning' as it is spoken

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [QUOTE] feeling fluent is not learning

beats.narration_text: Here's the event. When you hit something you can't yet do, a spark fires in the brain, and new connections grow. That growth is the memory.

production_viz.label: struggle → spark → connections grow
production_viz.mechanic: a navy cartoon brain meets a jagged 'mismatch' bolt (the thing you can't do); a navy spark flashes at the center; new navy dendrite lines branch outward and connect; label 'a spark fires'
production_viz.note: name a spark only — NO BDNF/dopamine/dendritic-spine biochemistry (card exclusion). 'That growth is the memory.'

beats.narration_text: Now remove the struggle. Let AI do the hard part. The worksheet fills in, but the spark never fires. No struggle, no spark. Nothing grows.
beats.scene_description: a crimson-outlined brain sits dark and empty; beside it a worksheet auto-fills itself line by line; no spark, no connections; a crimson label 'no struggle, no spark'

production_viz.label: remove the struggle → no spark
production_viz.mechanic: the worksheet fills itself in (navy checkmarks appear on their own); the crimson brain beside it stays dark and empty — no bolt, no spark, no branches; crimson label 'no struggle, no spark'
production_viz.note: the mirror of B05 — friction removed, trigger gone.

beats.new_visual_element: [COMPOSITE] worksheet fills itself, brain stays dark

beats.narration_text: Same hour, two brains. One lit up and wired itself. The other stayed dark while the page filled itself in. The finished homework looked identical.

production_viz.label: same hour, two brains
production_viz.mechanic: the hero compare — left navy brain full of grown connections and a lit spark; right crimson brain dark and empty; two identical worksheets sit below, both stamped 'done'; label 'identical homework'
production_viz.note: the compare move — the card's central visual object

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] two brains — grown vs dark, same homework

viz.label: wired vs dark, identical output
viz.note: the two brains side by side

beats.narration_text: Then the exam. No AI allowed. And the gap flipped. The group that struggled remembered. The group that outsourced it scored seventeen points lower.

production_viz.label: exam: the gap flips, -17 points
production_viz.mechanic: two bars under an 'exam (no AI)' label; now the navy struggle bar is higher, the crimson AI bar drops well below it; a crimson '-17 pts' tag; a faint ghost of B03's flipped ordering to show the reversal
production_viz.note: 17 points lower on the unassisted exam — verbatim from ch.97. The reversal of B03.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] exam bars — AI group 17 points lower

beats.narration_text: The artifact was perfect. The brain was unchanged. They'd borrowed the machine's ability instead of building their own, and the exam only tested theirs.
beats.scene_description: a flawless navy worksheet stamped 'A+' beside the dark crimson brain; the editor's pen rings the brain, not the paper; label 'artifact fine, brain unchanged'

production_viz.label: artifact fine, brain unchanged
production_viz.mechanic: a pristine navy worksheet with an 'A+' stamp on the left; the dark crimson empty brain on the right; a crimson HandRing lands around the BRAIN (not the paper) on 'unchanged'; label 'the exam tested the brain'
production_viz.note: 'The artifact was fine. The brain was unchanged.' — ch.97, near-verbatim.

beats.narration_text: So struggle isn't the price of learning. It is the learning. The best AI doesn't remove the struggle — it makes the struggle sharper.
beats.scene_description: the navy struggle-brain returns, spark lit; a small navy AI helper hands it a sharper mismatch / better question rather than the answer; the spark burns brighter; label 'sharper struggle, not none'

production_viz.label: good AI sharpens the struggle
production_viz.mechanic: the navy brain with its spark; a small navy 'AI' chip hands it a better question (a sharper bolt), not an answer; the spark grows brighter and one more connection forms; label 'sharper, not gone'
production_viz.note: ch.97: AI that makes the struggle more productive accelerates learning. No phase-gate/tier/product framing.

beats.new_visual_element: [COMPOSITE] AI hands a sharper question, spark brightens

beats.narration_text: Remove the struggle, and you remove the learning.

card.copy: Remove the struggle — you remove the learning.
card.sub: from The Reallocation Engine — the fundamental themes

beats.new_visual_element: [CARD] endcard — remove the struggle, remove the learning

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [CARD] endcard — remove the struggle, remove the learning
